states.name: Alabama
states.summary: Alabama offers tax incentives for data center development, but Birmingham enacted a 6-month moratorium on large data centers (>20 MW) in March 2026 while reviewing zoning rules.

localMoratoriums.description: City Council voted unanimously to enact a 6-month moratorium on applications for data centers requiring >20 MW of power. Exempts already-approved projects.

states.name: Alaska
states.summary: No statewide data center legislation, but Anchorage has adopted preemptive zoning regulations requiring conditional use permits and setbacks for large data centers.

localMoratoriums.description: Assembly adopted preemptive data center regulations: requires conditional use permits, 200-foot setbacks, visual screening, fire suppression, and noise mitigation for facilities using 20+ MW.

states.name: Arizona
states.summary: Arizona has introduced HB 2756 to prevent data center grid connection costs from being shifted to residential ratepayers. The state remains a major data center hub with active incentive programs.

localMoratoriums.description: Adopted ordinance restricting data center locations and requiring enhanced setbacks and noise standards.
Updated zoning code with data center-specific requirements including water use limits and noise buffers.
Enacted data center zoning overlay requiring conditional use permits for facilities over 10 MW.
Adopted data center-specific zoning rules for unincorporated areas including water consumption reporting.
Updated zoning code to restrict data center development near residential areas.
Enacted data center zoning restrictions addressing water and energy concerns in unincorporated areas.

states.name: Arkansas
states.summary: Arkansas provides tax incentives for data center investment, including reduced sales tax on qualifying equipment purchases.

states.name: California
states.summary: No statewide moratorium legislation, though some local jurisdictions have imposed water-use restrictions affecting proposed data center sites.

localMoratoriums.description: City Council unanimously adopted 45-day moratorium on data center applications, later extended through January 2027. Council voted to place a permanent citywide data center ban on the June 2026 ballot.

states.name: Colorado
states.summary: No statewide moratorium, but Denver has proposed a yearlong moratorium citing drought. Larimer County has an active moratorium, Logan County enacted and lifted one after passing regulations, and Weld County adopted new zoning requirements.

localMoratoriums.description: Proposed yearlong moratorium on new data center construction. Prompted by Stage 1 drought conditions and Denver Water's call for 20% water use reduction.
Moratorium on new data center permits in unincorporated areas, extended to August 25, 2026. County developing specific land-use regulations for data centers.
Six-month moratorium on data center projects. Lifted in late February 2026 after county adopted new regulations. County now expects a potential $15B data center proposal.

Updated land use code to allow data centers with new requirements. Permitted via admin review in industrial zones; requires special review in agricultural areas. Restrictions on noise, power, and water usage.

states.name: Connecticut
states.summary: Connecticut introduced SB 43 to update data center tax credits with a 5-year minimum use period requirement. Committee review ongoing.

localMoratoriums.description: Planning and Zoning Commission banned data centers larger than 12,500 sq ft after a year-long moratorium, blocking NE Edge's proposed 1.5M sq ft campus.

states.name: Delaware
states.summary: No statewide moratorium legislation, but New Castle County enacted comprehensive data center zoning regulations and DNREC denied a major data center under the Coastal Zone Act.

localMoratoriums.description: County Council unanimously approved first-in-state data center zoning regulations: 1,000-foot buffer from residential zones, ban on open-loop cooling, decommissioning fund requirements.
DNREC denied Project Washington data center under the Coastal Zone Act, classifying it as heavy industry due to 516 backup diesel generators. Appeals board unanimously upheld the denial.

states.name: Florida
states.summary: No statewide data center moratorium legislation. Florida continues to attract data center investment, particularly in the Miami and Jacksonville markets.

states.name: Georgia
states.summary: Georgia's state-level moratorium bills (HB 1012, SB 410, SB 408) all failed during the 2026 legislative session. However, 14 local jurisdictions have enacted their own moratoriums or restrictions, making Georgia one of the most active states for local-level data center regulation.

bills.title: End Data Center Tax Exemption Early

localMoratoriums.description: First Georgia county to enact a data center moratorium. Paused new data center approvals to study infrastructure impacts.
Extended moratorium through June 2026 on new data center construction permits.
Enacted moratorium on new data center construction to study water and grid impacts.
Paused data center permits pending infrastructure impact study.
Moratorium on new data center approvals to assess grid capacity.
Paused data center development pending review of water and energy impacts.
City moratorium on data center construction pending zoning updates.
Enacted moratorium that expired December 2025 after new zoning rules were adopted.
Moratorium on data center permits to study community and infrastructure impacts.
City moratorium on new data center construction.
Paused data center approvals pending review.
City moratorium on data center development.
Moratorium on data center construction in city limits.
Zoning restriction prohibiting data center development along the BeltLine corridor.

states.name: Hawaii
states.summary: No known data center moratorium or restriction legislation.

states.name: Idaho
states.summary: No statewide data center legislation, but Kootenai County enacted a moratorium (now expired, replaced by permanent aquifer protections) and Ada County is developing data center zoning amendments.

localMoratoriums.description: Commissioners approved a 182-day moratorium on data center building permits citing aquifer protection. Later replaced with permanent regulations banning data centers over the aquifer.
Developing Data Center Zoning Ordinance Amendment to regulate data centers and protect neighboring property owners and the environment.

states.name: Illinois
states.summary: Illinois introduced HB 4172 proposing a statewide data center moratorium. The separate POWER Act would shift energy costs to data center companies, incentivize clean energy, and limit water use. HB 4172 has not passed its originating chamber.

localMoratoriums.description: Enacted 6-month moratorium on new data center construction to study energy and water impacts.
Proposed moratorium on data center construction in Chicago suburb. Under city council review.

states.name: Indiana
states.summary: Indiana introduced HB 1333 taking a revenue-sharing approach, requiring data centers to share 1% of sales tax exemption savings with local governments rather than imposing a moratorium.

localMoratoriums.description: One of the first Indiana counties to enact a data center construction moratorium.
Moratorium on new data center permits pending infrastructure impact study.
Enacted moratorium on data center construction to study grid and water impacts.
Moratorium on data center development pending community impact review.
Most recent Indiana county to enact a data center construction moratorium.
Moratorium petition denied; county opted for a study committee instead.

states.name: Iowa
states.summary: No statewide moratorium, but Iowa has significant local action. Clarke and Johnson counties enacted moratoriums, and Linn County passed one of the nation's strictest data center zoning ordinances with 1,000-foot setbacks and water study requirements.

localMoratoriums.description: Temporary moratorium on construction of data centers in unincorporated areas due to absence of county zoning ordinances pertaining to data centers.
One-year moratorium on rezoning, site plans, and building permits for data centers in unincorporated areas. Concerns about water pollution, power usage, and facility siting.

One of the nation's strictest local data center zoning ordinances. Requires comprehensive water studies, 1,000-foot residential setbacks, noise and light limits, and community betterment fund contributions.

states.name: Kansas
states.summary: No statewide data center legislation, but Sedgwick County enacted a moratorium on data center permits extended through June 2026, and Miami County is considering a one-to-two-year moratorium.

localMoratoriums.description: 90-day moratorium on data center permits, extended to June 11, 2026. Two data center developers seeking 1,000+ acres in western Sedgwick County.
County Commission considering a moratorium lasting one to two years while developing regulations. Prompted by Osawatomie's $1B data center proposal.

states.name: Kentucky
states.summary: No statewide data center legislation, but Kentucky has become a hotbed of local moratoriums. Oldham, Scott, and Meade counties have active moratoriums, Simpson County restricted data centers to heavy industrial zones, and Louisville considered but shelved a moratorium.

localMoratoriums.description: Fiscal Court voted 4-2 to pass a 150-day moratorium on data center applications. Extended indefinitely until new regulations are approved. Triggered by a $6B, 600MW proposal that was later withdrawn.
Fiscal Court approved a six-month moratorium on data center applications as a proactive measure, despite no expressed developer interest.
Fiscal Court adopted countywide moratorium on new data center and large-scale IT infrastructure facility applications. Also rejected a rezoning proposal for a 138-acre data center site.
Fiscal Court restricted data storage centers to Heavy Industrial (I-2) zones only, requiring conditional use permits. Developer TenKey LandCo sued to overturn the ordinance.
Councilmember filed ordinance proposing a 6-month moratorium on data center approvals. Tabled indefinitely and expired without action in March 2026.

states.name: Louisiana
states.summary: No statewide moratorium legislation, but New Orleans enacted a one-year citywide ban on data center construction in January 2026. Louisiana continues to attract major data center investment in northern parishes.

localMoratoriums.description: City Council voted 6-0 to impose a one-year ban on data center construction, expansion, or establishment citywide. Could last up to 2 years.

states.name: Maine
states.summary: Maine's LD 307 passed the House 82-62 on April 6 and the Senate 19-13 on April 7, 2026. It awaits final enactment votes in both chambers. If enacted, it would be the first statewide data center moratorium in the US, banning facilities over 20 MW until November 2027 and creating a Data Center Coordination Council.

bills.title: Act to Impose a Moratorium on Large-Scale Data Centers
bills.status: Passed both chambers — awaiting enactment vote

localMoratoriums.description: City councilors fast-tracking six-month moratorium ordinance banning data centers to give the city time to adjust its Land Development Code.
City halted a proposed $300 million data center at Bates Mill after public outcry. Secretive plan collapsed in 6 days.
Halted a proposed data center amid public outcry over safety and water/resource use concerns.

states.name: Maryland
states.summary: Maryland introduced HB 120, prohibiting new data center construction unless the General Assembly enacts legislation related to co-location with power generation (including natural gas, nuclear, or SMRs). Still in committee.

bills.title: Data Center Construction and Power Co-Location Act

localMoratoriums.description: County Executive issued 180-day moratorium on data center permits. A 20-member task force delivered recommendations including prohibiting data centers in environmentally sensitive areas.
County Council unanimously passed bill freezing data center permits until Planning Board completes environmental and economic impact study. Expires 90 days after study received or by January 1, 2027.
County Council voted unanimously to pause data center permitting through end of 2026. Zoning text amendment introduced to restrict data centers to industrial zones.
Council passed Critical Digital Infrastructure Overlay Zone limiting data center development to ~2,600 acres (0.6% of county). For every new data center acre, county preserves five acres of farmland.
City Council President introduced legislation for one-year moratorium on data centers. Would restrict data centers to Office Industrial Campus and Industrial zones only.

states.name: Massachusetts
states.summary: Massachusetts is reviewing energy and water consumption requirements for data centers, with committee discussions ongoing. No moratorium bill has been filed.

localMoratoriums.description: City councilors voted unanimously to enact a one-year moratorium on data center development, the first of its kind in Massachusetts.
Planning Board voted unanimously to endorse ordinance restricting data centers in the Docklands Innovation District, a 100-acre redevelopment site.
Town will consider a data center moratorium at its annual town meeting in May 2026.

states.name: Michigan
states.summary: Michigan introduced a House bill package proposing a one-year moratorium preventing EGLE and local governments from issuing data center permits until April 2027. Governor Whitmer has publicly opposed moratorium efforts, making passage unlikely.

localMoratoriums.description: 12-month moratorium on data center construction permits.
Moratorium on data center development pending zoning review.
Paused data center permits to study infrastructure impacts.
6-month moratorium on data center construction.
Moratorium on new data center permits.
Paused data center approvals pending zoning ordinance update.
Moratorium on data center construction.
12-month moratorium on data center permits.
Moratorium expired after new zoning ordinance was adopted.
Moratorium on data center development.
City moratorium on data center construction permits.
Moratorium on new data center approvals.
6-month moratorium on data center permits.
City moratorium on data center construction.
Moratorium on data center development pending zoning updates.
12-month moratorium on data center construction permits.
Moratorium on new data center permits.
Proposed moratorium on data center construction. Under county board review.

states.name: Minnesota
states.summary: Minnesota enacted SF 608 (June 2025) restricting enterprise data centers to industrial districts statewide. Eagan became the first city to enact a local moratorium on data centers using more than 20 MW. SF 4298 proposes barring permits until a PUC study is complete.

localMoratoriums.description: One-year moratorium on development or expansion of data centers using more than 20 MW or located within 500 feet of residential uses. First Minnesota city to enact a data center moratorium.

states.name: Mississippi
states.summary: Mississippi offers tax incentives to attract data center development, including property tax exemptions for qualifying facilities.

states.name: Missouri
states.summary: No statewide moratorium legislation, but Missouri is a hotbed of local action. St. Charles enacted a citywide moratorium and is considering a permanent ban. Kansas City and St. Louis enacted zoning restrictions, and Columbia froze permits.

localMoratoriums.description: One-year moratorium on applications for new or expanded data centers, voted unanimously. Prompted by public backlash over secretive $1B 'Project Cumulus' proposal on 440-acre tract. City now considering permanent ban.
Mayor issued Executive Order establishing requirements for data center developers. Proposed rules would limit large-scale data centers to industrial zones with a 500,000 sq ft cap.

Reclassifies data centers from commercial to industrial facilities, limits them to specific industrial zoning districts, requires City Council approval for larger facilities and utility capacity confirmation.
120-day moratorium proposed on processing data center and battery energy storage land use applications in unincorporated areas. Referred to committee.
City Council voted 6-0 to direct staff to bring forward a six-month administrative delay on processing data center applications.

states.name: Montana
states.summary: No statewide moratorium despite significant lobbying efforts. Missoula County requires data center and crypto operations to offset 100% of electrical consumption with new renewable energy. Up to 14 data centers proposed statewide.

localMoratoriums.description: Permanent zoning regulations requiring any cryptocurrency mining or data center operation to purchase or develop enough new renewable energy to offset 100% of its electrical consumption.

states.name: Nebraska
states.summary: Nebraska has significant data center activity with Google facilities in Papillion, Omaha, and Lincoln, plus new mega-proposals. Gage County is examining data center zoning but no moratoriums enacted.

localMoratoriums.description: County supervisors reviewing regulations for industrial data centers. Moratorium discussed but county attorney advised a public hearing is legally required first.

states.name: Nevada
states.summary: Nevada, particularly the Reno-Sparks area, is a major data center market with state tax abatements for qualifying facilities.

localMoratoriums.description: Administrative interpretation established data centers as conditional use in certain zones, requiring all data centers to go through conditional use permit process.

states.name: New Hampshire
states.summary: New Hampshire's HB 1265 proposes a one-year moratorium on data center construction. Public hearing held February 11, 2026. Related bill SB 439 on municipal data center zoning authority had a House committee hearing on April 7.

states.name: New Jersey
states.summary: New Jersey is reviewing data center impacts on the electrical grid and water resources, with legislative hearings underway.

localMoratoriums.description: First NJ municipality to ban data centers. Passed Ordinance 10-2026 prohibiting the construction and operation of data centers.
Township Council introduced ordinance to eliminate data centers from the Hexa Builders Redevelopment Plan, reversing a June 2025 zoning change.
Town Council voted unanimously to approve first reading of ordinance banning data centers in all zones. Land Use Board voted to support the ban.

states.name: New Mexico
states.summary: No known statewide data center moratorium or restriction legislation.

states.name: New York
states.summary: New York has proposed S9144/A10141, a 3-year moratorium on permits for data centers using 20 MW+. Both bills referred to Environmental Conservation committees in February 2026 but have not moved since. The state previously enacted a 2-year moratorium on proof-of-work crypto mining in 2022.

states.name: North Carolina
states.summary: North Carolina is reviewing data center energy demands as rapid growth in the Charlotte and Triangle regions strains the electrical grid. No state moratorium bill has been filed, but multiple local moratoriums have been enacted.

localMoratoriums.description: Moratorium on data center construction permits.
County moratorium on data center development pending zoning review.
City moratorium on data center construction.
County moratorium on data center permits.
Moratorium on data center construction in city limits.
Proposed moratorium on data center construction. Under town council review.
Proposed moratorium on data center development.
Considering a moratorium on data center construction.

states.name: North Dakota
states.summary: No statewide moratorium, but Morton, Oliver, and Mercer counties have active moratoriums on data center construction. A state Data Center Model Ordinance Working Group completed a model ordinance in January 2026.

localMoratoriums.description: Moratorium on data centers and cryptocurrency mining facilities. No facilities may be permitted until the county develops and adopts siting and performance standards.
180-day moratorium on data center construction. Commission developing rules about where data centers can be located.
One-year moratorium on permitting for new AI data centers, passed 3-2. NextEra subsidiary had secured options on approximately 530 acres.

states.name: Ohio
states.summary: Ohio legislators are reviewing the impact of large-scale data center development on the electrical grid and local communities. No state moratorium bill filed in 2026, but multiple local moratoriums are active.

localMoratoriums.description: 12-month moratorium on data center construction permits.
Moratorium expired after zoning ordinance updates were completed.
6-month moratorium enacted after reversing an earlier outright ban on data centers.
90-day moratorium on data center permits.

states.name: Oklahoma
states.summary: Oklahoma SB 1488 requiring community impact assessments stalled in committee. Tulsa enacted a nine-month moratorium on new data center construction through December 2026. Sand Springs faced recall efforts after rezoning for a Google data center.

localMoratoriums.description: City Council unanimously approved a nine-month moratorium on new data center construction through December 31, 2026. Exempts existing projects.
Google data center rezoning triggered recall effort against entire City Council and a lawsuit challenging the land deal. 1,600+ signatures gathered.

states.name: Oregon
states.summary: Oregon is reviewing data center water usage and environmental requirements, particularly in the Dalles region where Google operates large facilities. No moratorium bill filed in 2026.

localMoratoriums.description: Enterprise zone tax break moratorium affecting data center development in the region where Google operates large facilities.

states.name: Pennsylvania
states.summary: Pennsylvania Sen. Katie Muth is planning a three-year moratorium on hyperscale data center development. Separate data center regulation bills have advanced in the PA House. During the pause, agencies would study environmental, energy, and community impacts.

localMoratoriums.description: Three county commissioners unanimously denied Talen Energy's request to rezone 800+ acres from agricultural to industrial for data center development near Montour Preserve.

states.name: Rhode Island
states.summary: No statewide moratorium legislation. Smithfield is considering a data center ban after a large woodland development was proposed. State legislators have introduced data center regulation bills.

localMoratoriums.description: Town Council unanimously agreed to hold a public hearing to amend zoning ordinance defining and potentially banning data centers. Prompted by a proposal on several hundred acres of woodland.

states.name: South Carolina
states.summary: South Carolina's H. 5286 bars state and local governments from granting permits, approvals, or incentives for new data centers until January 2028. Covers rezoning, special use permits, building permits, and economic incentives. Filed March 2026, referred to Ways and Means.

bills.title: Data Center Permit and Incentive Freeze

states.name: South Dakota
states.summary: South Dakota's moratorium bills (SB 232, HB 1301) both failed to advance. However, SB 135 passed — it prohibits the state from limiting local government authority to regulate or ban data centers, effectively empowering municipal moratoriums.

states.name: Tennessee
states.summary: No statewide moratorium, but Moore County has proposed a 2-year moratorium on AI data center and crypto mining permits. Memphis faces controversy over xAI's unpermitted gas turbines.

localMoratoriums.description: Planning Commission recommended a 2-year moratorium on AI data center, cryptocurrency mining, and similar high-impact computing facility permits.

states.name: Texas
states.summary: Texas has no statewide data center moratorium and state law constrains county moratorium authority. San Marcos denied a data center over water concerns. Hood County and Hays County moratorium proposals were rejected or tabled.

localMoratoriums.description: City Council voted 5-2 to deny a land use map change for CloudBurst Data Centers. Concerns centered on water use (up to 70,000 gallons/day) during drought conditions on the Edwards Aquifer.

states.name: Utah
states.summary: Utah offers tax incentives for data center development and has seen significant growth in the Salt Lake City area.

localMoratoriums.description: City Council unanimously voted to deny a rezoning application for a 66,000 sq ft data center, citing need to complete economic master plan first.

states.name: Vermont
states.summary: Vermont's S.205 proposes a moratorium on AI data centers requiring 100 MW+ until July 2030 — the longest proposed moratorium period nationally. Referred to Senate Finance and Natural Resources & Energy committees. Has not passed originating chamber.

localMoratoriums.description: Voters approved a five-year moratorium on AI and crypto data center construction at Town Meeting Day by show of hands.

states.name: Virginia
states.summary: Virginia's HB 1515 moratorium was carried over to the 2027 session (effectively dead for 2026). However, HB 2084 directing the SCC to review data center rate classifications was enacted and signed by the governor. Over 60 data center bills were considered in 2026. Virginia remains the world's largest data center market.

localMoratoriums.description: Eliminated by-right data center development. New facilities require special exception approval. Home to the world's largest concentration of data centers.
Eliminated by-right data center development, requiring conditional use permits for all new facilities.
Digital Gateway data center rezoning was invalidated by courts. County adopted stricter zoning requirements for data center development.

states.name: Washington
states.summary: Washington state is reviewing data center energy and water consumption, particularly in areas served by hydroelectric power in central Washington.

states.name: West Virginia
states.summary: West Virginia offers tax incentives for data center development and is marketing its low energy costs to attract facilities.

states.name: Wisconsin
states.summary: Wisconsin's Democratic caucus proposed a broad moratorium on data center construction requiring 100% renewable energy, prevailing wages, no subsidies, and a planning authority before construction can proceed. The bill faces a Republican legislative majority.

bills.title: Data Center Community Protection and Moratorium Act

localMoratoriums.description: Passed the nation's first anti-data center referendum, banning data center construction by public vote.
City moratorium on data center construction.
County committee recommended moratorium for three townships. Under board review.
Township moratorium on data center construction in the Driftless Area.
Township moratorium on data center development in the Driftless Area.

states.name: Wyoming
states.summary: Wyoming offers low energy costs and tax incentives favorable to data center development.
